title: Pronouncing -ing, I'll, and Gonna
learningGoal: Master the pronunciation of the -ing ending, future contractions (I'll, he'll, won't), and the casual form 'gonna'.
instructions: 進行形の「-ing」の発音、willの短縮形（I'll, won't）、そしてネイティブがよく使う「gonna（going toの短縮）」の発音を練習します。マイクに向かってリピートしてください。(Practice the -ing ending, future contractions, and 'gonna'. Repeat into the microphone.)

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Native speakers often drop the hard 'g' at the end. It sounds like 'ee-tin'.
Wa-chin. Don't add an extra vowel at the end.

targetPhrasesOrWords.baseTranslation: 私は〜するつもりだ（I will の短縮）
彼は〜するつもりだ（He will の短縮）
彼女は〜するつもりだ（She will の短縮）
〜しないだろう（will not の短縮）
targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: This is tricky! It sounds like 'w-OH-nt'. Make your lips round. Do NOT say 'want' (ウォント).

targetPhrasesOrWords.baseTranslation: 〜する予定だ（going to のカジュアルな発音）
targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: In conversation, 'going to' usually sounds like 'gun-uh' or 'gah-nuh'.
targetPhrasesOrWords.commonMispronunciation: Saying 'gonna' clearly as two separate words like 'go-na'.

expectedPronunciationText: eating running watching I'll he'll she'll won't going to gonna doing

feedbackRules.onPass: Great job! Your '-ing' sounds very natural, and you nailed 'won't' and 'gonna'.
feedbackRules.onFail: Please try again. Be careful not to add an extra 'gu' sound at the end of the -ing words.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort! Remember that 'won't' has a round 'oh' sound. Make sure it doesn't sound like 'want'.

adminCorrectAnswerSet.exactCorrectTranscript: eating running watching I'll he'll she'll won't going to gonna doing
acceptedVariants: eating running watching ill hell shell wont going to gonna doing
eating running watching I will he will she will will not going to gonna doing
adminCorrectAnswerSet.nativeScriptReference: eating running watching I'll he'll she'll won't going to gonna doing
adminCorrectAnswerSet.transliteration: eetin runin wachin ile heel sheel wohnt goin too gunuh dooin

audioAssets.audioTranscript: eating, running, watching, I'll, he'll, she'll, won't, going to, gonna, doing

hintText: To sound like a native, try to say 'gonna' instead of 'going to' when you are speaking naturally.
